question: What is Novo Nordisk's technical/options/quant setup?

findings.claim: Options market shows bullish bias despite the crash: put-to-call volume ratio was 0.16 (heavily skewed to calls), and put-to-call open interest ratio was 0.67. This suggests options traders are betting on a rebound rather than further decline.
findings.data: Put volume: 679, Call volume: 4,400. Put/call volume ratio: 0.16. Put/call OI ratio: 0.67 (across 51 outstanding contracts for Apr 17 expiry, data from Macroaxis). 30-day implied volatility (calls): 0.5189 as of Jan 30, 2026. IV percentile: 15.67% (Low) as of Jan 8, 2026.

findings.claim: Short interest is low and declining: only 0.84% of shares short (27M shares), down from 31.7M previously. Days-to-cover ratio is 1.87. This is NOT a crowded short — there is no short squeeze setup.
findings.data: Short interest decreased from 31.69M to 26.97M shares. 0.84% of outstanding shares sold short. 0.79% of float sold short. Days-to-cover: 1.87x average daily volume. For context, a typical short squeeze candidate would have >15% of float short and days-to-cover >5.

findings.claim: Barchart's technical rating is 100% Sell with strong continuation signal, while analyst consensus is Hold with average price target of $42-$51 (14-38% upside from current). Wide dispersion in targets ($31-$160) reflects extreme uncertainty.
findings.data: Analyst targets by source: StockAnalysis: $51 avg (4 analysts, Hold), TipRanks: $42.80 avg ($40-47 range, 7 analysts, Hold), Investing.com: $47.25 avg ($39.85-63.60, 11 analysts), Public.com: Buy consensus (6 analysts). Guggenheim recently downgraded to Neutral. Benzinga shows widest range: $31-$160 (14 analysts).
